summary: Brunei's posture is at the readiness-assessment stage. Cyber Security Brunei, the national cyber security agency, co-hosted the country's first Quantum Technology and Post-Quantum Cryptography workshop with Malaysian counterparts, bringing together national agencies, regulators and banks to assess Brunei Darussalam's readiness and align national effort towards post-quantum cryptography migration.
govActors: CSB | Cyber Security Brunei, national cyber security agency convening national post-quantum cryptography readiness work
provenance: CSB workshop + readiness assessment + PQC migration alignment: Cyber Security Brunei press release (ingested T1)
dominantProcess omitted: readiness/awareness posture only, no standards camp (NIST/ISO/ETSI) named - grey beats wrong
No standardFamilies, algorithms, hybrid stance, timeline or target stated
CSB Facebook post excluded (non-institutional medium, duplicate of the press release)

dominantProcess re-classified NIST by best-available signal (low): CSB's first PQC readiness workshop (with Malaysia's NACSA/PTPKM) is an awareness/readiness posture with no sovereign Bruneian scheme, so it defaults to the de facto international (NIST) camp. Thin readiness-stage signal - provisional, flagged for review
Model 2026-06-14 (engaged-unaligned, Low): Minimal evidence; no national adoption found. Engaged but uncommitted.
